key_findings: Day-1 concurrency envelopes (DNS:250, HTTP:75, Enrich:25, Ingest:15 tx) are confirmed as the correct operational starting point for the 200k chunk; no tuning required before first pass
Redis Bloom filter must be deployed and pre-warmed before first production write — this is the single highest-risk pre-flight step; skipping it will cause duplicate write rates to exceed 0.5% target
Write order must be enforced as a code contract: Neo4j writes BEFORE ClickHouse writes — this prevents ClickHouse orphan rows on Neo4j failure
Per-batch cross-store reconciliation (validate lane) must fire after every ingest batch, not end-of-run — early detection prevents ingesting millions of corrupt records before discovery
Retry logic must be pinned at the tool layer (dnsx/httpx task level), not the queue layer — queue-level retry causes DLQ thrashing and queue depth inflation under error conditions
Quarantine protocol must be in place before first run — all integrity-class failures require forensic replay path; silent discard is not acceptable
ClickHouse reconciliation queries must use FINAL keyword — without FINAL, ReplacingMergeTree dedup lag causes false mismatch alarms in validate lane
Checkpoint must be written BEFORE datastore write, not after — post-write checkpoints create double-write exposure on retry

decisions.title: Retry pinned at tool layer
decisions.summary: dnsx/httpx/ingest retries execute in-process at tool level; failures beyond max retries escalate to DLQ — not re-enqueued to primary queue
decisions.rationale: Queue-level retry causes queue depth inflation and backpressure instability; tool-layer retry is bounded and does not affect AWSEM queue metrics

decisions.title: Neo4j-first write order enforced as code contract
decisions.summary: ClickHouse write is skipped if Neo4j write fails; Neo4j is the graph authority
decisions.rationale: Prevents ClickHouse orphan rows that would appear in telemetry but have no corresponding graph node; validate lane reconciliation would flag these as mismatch

decisions.title: Checkpoint written before datastore write (write-ahead)
decisions.summary: WAL checkpoint record created with status=PENDING before any datastore write; updated to COMPLETE after both writes succeed
decisions.rationale: Post-write checkpoints allow double-write on retry; pre-write checkpoints guarantee safe replay at the cost of a small idempotency overhead (absorbed by MERGE semantics)

decisions.title: Per-batch validate lane reconciliation with 30s settle delay
decisions.summary: Validate lane fires after each ingest batch; 30s settle delay before ClickHouse query to allow ReplacingMergeTree merge to complete
decisions.rationale: Without settle delay, FINAL keyword may not fully resolve all dedup operations in high-insert-rate conditions; 30s is conservative and safe for current batch sizes

decisions.title: Quarantine-not-discard for all failure classes
decisions.summary: All failed items written to artifacts/quarantine/{batch_id}_{lane}_{ts}/ with full forensic context
decisions.rationale: Intelligence value of quarantined items may be recoverable; silent discard is irreversible and may cause coverage gaps in subdomain intelligence corpus

decisions.title: Bloom filter FPR at 0.01%
decisions.summary: Redis Bloom filter configured for 10M expected insertions at 0.01% false positive rate; ~240MB bit array, 14 hash functions
decisions.rationale: 0.01% FPR at 10M scale = ~1,000 false positives (valid subdomains incorrectly skipped); recoverable via DLQ replay; lower FPR would double memory footprint

decisions.title: DNS-gate before HTTP lane activation
decisions.summary: HTTP lane does not activate until DNS validation pass: error rate ≤2%, dupe rate ≤0.5%, link accuracy ≥99.5%
decisions.rationale: Prevents HTTP probing on unconfirmed DNS data; eliminates dangling HTTP graph edges with no DNS node parent

decisions.title: Per-lane DLQ with class-specific TTL
decisions.rationale: Longer TTL on ingest/validate DLQ because those items require manual forensic investigation; shorter TTL on DNS/HTTP because re-scanning is a valid recovery path

validate_lane.clickhouse_query: SELECT count(*) FROM subdomains FINAL WHERE ingested_batch_id = %(batch_id)s

consistency_verification.clickhouse_query_template: SELECT count(*) FROM subdomains FINAL WHERE ingested_batch_id = %(batch_id)s
consistency_verification.required_field: ingested_batch_id on both Neo4j node properties and ClickHouse rows

risk_model.title: Bloom filter not pre-warmed
risk_model.mitigation: Block production start on warm confirmation; verify item count matches known subdomain inventory

risk_model.title: ClickHouse orphan rows on Neo4j failure
risk_model.mitigation: Enforced Neo4j-first write order; validate lane cross-store check catches post-facto

risk_model.title: DLQ growing unbounded
risk_model.mitigation: Per-lane DLQ TTL enforcement; ORS alert on DLQ depth > 2,000 items

risk_model.title: ClickHouse dedup lag causing false reconciliation alarms
risk_model.mitigation: 30s settle delay before reconciliation query; FINAL keyword in all count queries

risk_model.title: Checkpoint store (Redis) failure
risk_model.mitigation: Redis AOF persistence; SQLite WAL fallback; checkpoint failure = halt ingest

risk_model.title: Quarantine directory growing without cleanup
risk_model.mitigation: 30-day retention with auto-archive; ORS alert at 10k items

risk_model.title: Bloom filter false positive silently suppressing valid subdomains
risk_model.mitigation: At 0.01% FPR and 10M items: ~1,000 false positives; recoverable via DLQ replay after Bloom filter reset

recommended_actions.action: Deploy and warm Redis Bloom filter before first production write — block start on warm confirmation
Enforce Neo4j-first write order in ingest controller code with explicit code contract comment
Create artifacts/quarantine/ directory and implement quarantine writer before first run
Implement validate lane reconciliation with FINAL keyword and 30s settle delay; add ingested_batch_id to both Neo4j and ClickHouse write paths
Add per-lane DLQ depth ORS monitor: WARNING 500, CRITICAL 2000
Run 200k chunk DNS-only first; gate HTTP on DNS validation pass (error<=2%, dupe<=0.5%)
After successful 200k full-pipeline cycle: step DNS concurrency 250→350; monitor 2h before next step
Export per-lane throughput and DLQ metrics to Mattermost OPS channel after each 5,000-item batch

assumptions: A1: Oracle produces SHA-256 hash files alongside source chunk data for source integrity verification
A2: Redis deployed on Controller with AOF persistence enabled
A3: AWSEM supports per-partition queue depth signals accessible by ORS
A4: ClickHouse version >= 21.6 (FINAL keyword available); if not, use GROUP BY dedup in reconciliation
A5: ingested_batch_id field is added to Neo4j node properties and ClickHouse rows during ingest write path
A6: Bloom filter false positive rate of 0.01% is acceptable (~1,000 valid subdomains may be incorrectly skipped at 10M scale; recoverable)
